'''Mark Edmondson''' (born 3 November 1979), also known by the [[nickname]] of '''"Edmo"''', is an English former [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s.

==Background==
Edmondson was born in [[Lancaster, Lancashire]], England. He attended [[Morecambe Community High School]].

==Career==
Edmondson signed as a professional for Super League champions '''[[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]]''' at the age of 17 and played with the club until 2005 before leaving to play in Australia for the [[Sydney Roosters]] in the NRL.

Edmondson's time at St Helens coincided with one of the most successful periods in the history of the club. He was a member of the team that won the [[1999 Super League Grand Final|Grand Final]] against Bradford in October 1999.  The club retained their Super League title in [[2000 Super League Grand Final|2000]] beating [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] 29–16.<ref name="Super League IV Grand Final">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/photo_galleries/972794.stm|title=Super League IV Grand Final|publisher=BBC|date=14 October 2000|access-date=2009-07-16}}</ref> They won the [[Challenge Cup]] in 2001; 13–6 over Bradford,<ref name="2001 Challenge Cup Final">{{cite web|url=http://www.saints.org.uk/saints.org.uk/home/viewpage.php?page_id=7&seq=77|title=2001 Challenge Cup Final Final|publisher=Saints Heritage Society|date=28 April 2001|accessdate=2009-07-16}}</ref> with the final held at [[Twickenham Stadium]] for the first time,<ref name="RFL Remains Bullish On Twickenham">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/challenge_cup/4864638.stm|title=RFL Remains Bullish On Twickenham|publisher=BBC|date=8 March 2006|access-date=2009-07-16}}</ref> and the [[2001 World Club Challenge]], earning a 20–18 win over the Broncos.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/6380851.stm|title=World Club Challenge Chris Joynt|publisher=BBC|date=22 February 2007|access-date=2009-07-16}}</ref> The club regained the Super League title in the [[2002 Super League Grand Final|2002 Grand Final]],<ref name="Super League VII Table">{{cite web|url=http://www.saints.org.uk/saints.org.uk/home/viewpage.php?page_id=24&tabledate=2002|title=Super League VII Table|publisher=Saints Heritage Society|date=26 September 2002|accessdate=2010-08-12}}</ref> [[Sean Long]] snatching a last minute 19–18 win over the [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]] with a [[drop—goal]].<ref name="2002 Super League Grand Final">{{cite web|url=http://www.saints.org.uk/saints.org.uk/home/viewpage.php?page_id=7&seq=17|title=2002 Super League Grand Final|publisher=Saints Heritage Society|date=19 October 2002|accessdate=2009-07-16}}</ref> In 2004, St Helens defeated Wigan 32–16 at the [[Millennium Stadium]], [[Cardiff]] in front of a capacity crowd of 73,734 people to win the [[Challenge Cup]],.<ref name="2004 Challenge Cup Final">{{cite web|url=http://www.saints.org.uk/saints.org.uk/home/viewpage.php?page_id=7&seq=4509|title=2004 Challenge Cup Final Final|publisher=Saints Heritage Society|date=15 May 2004|accessdate=2009-07-16}}</ref>

Edmondson played alongside some of Britain's greatest players such as [[Paul Sculthorpe]], [[Sean Long]], [[Keiron Cunningham]], [[Paul Newlove]], [[Chris Joynt]], [[Tommy Martyn]] and [[Paul Wellens]].

Edmondson represented the England International rugby league team and toured South Africa, Tonga, Fiji and Australia in 2002.

Edmondson was selected for the England team in 2005 but withdrew to undergo shoulder corrective surgery.

In 2006, Edmondson moved to Australia to join the [[Sydney Roosters]] coached by [[Ricky Stuart]].

After pre-season shoulder reconstruction, Edmondson made his début for the [[Sydney Roosters]] against the [[Brisbane Broncos]] at Aussie Stadium, Sydney. Edmondson suffered further injuries and his contract with the [[Sydney Roosters]] was terminated in 2007.

After a period of rehabilitation for his injuries, Edmondson returned to England and signed for the [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]]. He made his début against the Leeds Rhinos in round one of the Super League in 2007.

After further time out of the game with injury, Edmondson signed for the Castleford Tigers on 30 October 2007. He failed the club's medical and he retired from rugby league at the age of 28.

After retirement, Edmondson pursued courses in sport performance and established a consultancy to service high performance sport.
